column1: MLS-C01,"Domain 2: Exploratory Data Analysis","Exploratory Data Analysis","## MLS-C01 Domain 2: Exploratory Data Analysis
This domain covers statistics, visualization, feature engineering, dimensionality reduction, and data preparation for ML.

column3: Pixel arrays (H x W x C)

column1: - **Right-skewed (positive):** Mean > Median > Mode. Fix: Log transform, sqrt, Box-Cox

column3: ROC, PR Curve, Confusion Matrix, Learning Curve

column1: - **Learning Curve:** Training vs. validation error (diagnose over/underfitting)
- **PR Curve:** Better than ROC for imbalanced data
- **Residual Plot:** Predicted vs. residuals (check regression assumptions)
- Log transform: Reduce right skewness (requires x > 0)
- Polynomial features: Capture non-linear relationships in linear models

column4: Captures target relationship; risk of overfitting

column1: Pipeline: Lowercase -> Remove punctuation -> Tokenize -> Stop words -> Stem/Lemmatize -> Vectorize
- Bag of Words: Word counts (sparse, loses order)
- Rolling statistics: Moving average, rolling std
- Date features: Day of week, month, quarter, holiday
- Always consider: Add missing indicator column

column3: Outliers ARE the signal (fraud, anomaly detection)

column1: **Critical:** For fraud/anomaly detection, NEVER remove outliers - they're what you're detecting!
**PCA:** Requires standardized data. Choose K components explaining 95% variance.
- Apply SMOTE to TRAINING data only (never test/validation)
- USE: Precision, Recall, F1, AUC-ROC, AUC-PR
- **Why it matters:** ARIMA and many models assume stationarity
- **ACF cuts off at lag q** -> MA(q) model
- **PACF cuts off at lag p** -> AR(p) model
- Transfer learning: Pre-trained model, fine-tune with few labels
- **Pre-training:** Class Imbalance, Difference in Proportions, KL Divergence
- **Post-training:** Disparate Impact, Accuracy Difference, Treatment Equality
- **""Right-skewed data""** -> Log transform; use median not mean
- **""Time series gaps""** -> Forward fill or interpolation
- **""Fraud detection - remove outliers?""** -> NO (they're the signal)
- **""Reduce impact without removing""** -> Winsorize or log transform
- **""High cardinality categorical""** -> Target encoding or embedding
- **""Highly correlated features""** -> Remove one or PCA
- **""Don't use accuracy""** -> Use F1, AUC-ROC, AUC-PR
- **Parquet** for analytics. **RecordIO** for SageMaker Pipe mode.
- **t-SNE is for visualization ONLY** - not for feature extraction.
- **SMOTE on training data only** - never on test/validation.
- **Accuracy is misleading** for imbalanced data. Use F1, AUC-PR.
- **Random Cut Forest** = AWS anomaly detection (streaming + batch).
- **XGBoost handles missing values natively** (learns best split direction).
- **Log transform** requires positive values. Use log(x+1) for zeros.
- **One-Hot encoding trap:** Drop one column (N-1) to avoid multicollinearity.
- **Time series: NEVER random split.** Always temporal ordering.
- **Ground Truth Active Learning** reduces labeling cost by up to 70%.
This covers all objectives in MLS-C01 Domain 2:
- **Visualization** - Chart selection, ML-specific plots, AWS tools
- **Feature Engineering** - Scaling, encoding, text, time series
- **Missing Data** - Types, imputation strategies, algorithm-specific handling
- **Dimensionality Reduction** - PCA, t-SNE, LDA, feature selection, regularization
- **Imbalanced Data** - SMOTE, class weights, evaluation metrics
- **Time Series** - Components, stationarity, ACF/PACF, DeepAR, Forecast
- **Data Labeling** - Ground Truth, active learning, annotation
- **""Features highly correlated""** -> Remove one (> 0.9) or PCA to decorrelate
- **""VIF > 10""** -> Multicollinearity; remove feature, combine, or use Ridge (L2)
- **""Feature selection during training""** -> Embedded methods (Lasso, tree importance)
- **""t-SNE for model input?""** -> NO - visualization only (stochastic, not invertible)
- **""99% class A, 1% class B""** -> SMOTE + class weights + F1/AUC-PR (not accuracy)
- **""Create synthetic minority samples""** -> SMOTE (interpolates between K nearest)
- **""When to apply SMOTE?""** -> After split, on training data ONLY
- **""Evaluation for imbalanced""** -> AUC-PR (severe) or F1 (moderate) - NEVER accuracy
- **""Extreme imbalance (0.01%)""** -> Anomaly detection (Isolation Forest, One-Class SVM)
- **""Label images cost-effectively""** -> Ground Truth + Active Learning (70% cost reduction)
- **""Sensitive data labeling""** -> Private workforce (not Mechanical Turk)
- **""Few labeled examples""** -> Semi-supervised or transfer learning
- **""Detect bias before training""** -> Clarify pre-training metrics (CI, DPL, KL)
- **""Detect bias after training""** -> Clarify post-training metrics (Disparate Impact)
- **""ARIMA parameters""** -> PACF for p, ACF for q, d = differences needed
- **""Forecast multiple related series""** -> DeepAR (trains on all simultaneously)
- **""Time series split""** -> Never random; temporal ordering
- **""Distribution of single variable""** -> Histogram or Box Plot
- **PCA requires standardization.** Tested repeatedly. Without it, largest-scale feature dominates.
- **SMOTE only on training data.** Split first, then SMOTE. Test must reflect real distribution.
- **Trees don't need scaling.** XGBoost, RF use splits (thresholds), not distances.
- **Random Cut Forest** = AWS anomaly detection. Kinesis Analytics (streaming) + SageMaker (batch).
- **t-SNE = visualization only.** Stochastic, not invertible, doesn't preserve global structure.
- **L1 (Lasso) = feature selection.** Drives coefficients to zero. L2 (Ridge) shrinks but doesn't eliminate.
- **Box-Cox requires positive values.** Use Yeo-Johnson for zeros/negatives.
- **ADF test:** p < 0.05 = stationary (counterintuitive - low p = good).
- **Ground Truth Active Learning** = 70% cost reduction for labeling.
- **Median** is robust to outliers; mean is not. Use median for skewed data.
- **One-Hot trap:** N-1 columns to avoid multicollinearity in linear models.
- **DeepAR** trains on multiple series simultaneously. Probabilistic forecasts (quantiles).

column2: +-- Distribution: Histogram, Box Plot, QQ Plot, Violin
+-- ML-Specific: ROC, PR Curve, Learning Curve, Confusion Matrix
+-- Time Series: Line plot, ACF/PACF plots
+-- AWS: QuickSight (BI), Studio Notebooks, Data Wrangler
+-- Numerical: Scaling (Z-score, Min-Max), Transforms (log, Box-Cox), Binning
+-- Categorical: One-Hot, Label, Target, Frequency, Embedding, Hashing
+-- Time Series: Lag, rolling stats, date features, differencing
+-- Special: XGBoost handles natively, missing indicator column
+-- Detection: Z-score, IQR, Isolation Forest, RCF
+-- Key Rule: Don't remove if outliers ARE the signal (fraud)
+-- Curse of Dimensionality: More features -> need more data
+-- Algorithmic: Class weights, scale_pos_weight, threshold tuning
+-- Rule: SMOTE on training only, split BEFORE resampling
+-- Stationarity: ADF test, differencing, log transform
+-- Ground Truth: Managed labeling, Active Learning (70% cost reduction)
+-- Active Learning: Auto-label confident, human for uncertain

column1: +-- Pre-Training: Class Imbalance, DPL, KL Divergence

column4: Mean vs. Median, Pearson vs. Spearman, skewness
Histogram, Heatmap, QQ Plot, Learning Curve
Standardization (linear), Normalization (NN), NOT needed for trees
IQR, RCF (streaming), Winsorize, keep for fraud
ADF test, differencing, ACF/PACF, temporal split
